checkpoint_policy: Use existing script checkpoints and rerun only missing/forced rows.
pass_interpretation: Keep if false-signal control improves without lowering rare-state/real-data recovery.
stop_condition: Drop or demote if it only improves pure-null metrics while harming labeled real data.

checkpoint_policy: Use fixed random_state family; record all PC diagnostics.
pass_interpretation: Keep if finite-sample edge control improves without power loss.
stop_condition: Demote to supplement if effect is negligible against MP-only.

checkpoint_policy: Record runtime/memory because calibration may be expensive.
pass_interpretation: Keep as optional if it improves calibration but costs runtime.
stop_condition: Do not make it default if runtime prevents workstation-scale use.

checkpoint_policy: Run on the five labeled datasets only.
pass_interpretation: Keep if it reduces parameter dependence without annotation penalty.
stop_condition: Demote if fixed HVG is non-inferior and simpler.

checkpoint_policy: Pair repeats with the same seed family as official Seurat baselines.
pass_interpretation: Keep if stability/annotation Pareto improves or remains defensible.
stop_condition: Do not claim stability advantage if strongest baseline remains higher.

checkpoint_policy: Report full power curve by prevalence/effect, not only the best cell.
pass_interpretation: Keep if rare-state recovery improves without overclustering.
stop_condition: Demote if weak-effect/low-prevalence settings are unstable.

checkpoint_policy: Separate no-call correctness from forced-clustering performance.
pass_interpretation: Keep as a core claim if false-cluster control is clearly improved.
stop_condition: Do not frame no-call as failure if the null/low-signal contract is met.

pass_interpretation: Keep if batch-driven PCs decrease without biological signal loss.
stop_condition: Demote if residualization removes condition/state biology.
